_meta.note: First real regression pins for the controlled-method specification DSL exemplar (manuscript/03_results.md). Every value is re-derived by calling the real src/methods_dsl functions (compile_method, run_all_gates, demo_chain_report) on the two shipped worked example methods (PBSPreparation, SensorCalibrationSweep) -- never copied from the manuscript. The two full SHA-256 plan hashes are the strongest pins: compile_method uses a canonical sorted-key JSON encoding + Kahn's-algorithm topological sort, so the hash is byte-exact and reproducible across processes/platforms; any change to a step, its order, kind, target, or the method target/version flips the hash. The manuscript displays only the first 12 hex chars ({{PBS_PLAN_HASH}} / {{CALIBRATION_PLAN_HASH}}); these pins bind the full digest so sub-display drift is also caught.

pbs_plan_hash.reason: Full SHA-256 plan hash of the compiled PBSPreparation method. Deterministic per compiler.py design principle #5 (same source + same options -> same plan hash). Manuscript shows first 12 hex ({{PBS_PLAN_HASH}} = 313b9b17de98) in the compiled-plan summary table; this pin binds the full digest.

calibration_plan_hash.reason: Full SHA-256 plan hash of the compiled SensorCalibrationSweep method (mixed AUTOMATED/HUMAN target). Manuscript shows first 12 hex ({{CALIBRATION_PLAN_HASH}} = d89cced19be6); this pin binds the full digest.

pbs_step_count.reason: Number of scheduled steps in the compiled PBSPreparation plan ({{PBS_STEP_COUNT}} in the compiled-plan summary table). Exact-match count. Re-derived via len(compile_method(...).steps), also plotted by fig:step_counts.

calibration_step_count.reason: Number of scheduled steps in the compiled SensorCalibrationSweep plan ({{CALIBRATION_STEP_COUNT}} in the compiled-plan summary table). Exact-match count.

total_gates_passed.reason: Total staged-gate evaluations passing across both methods: {{TOTAL_GATES_PASSED}} of {{TOTAL_GATES_RUN}}. run_all_gates returns all four gates (structural, semantic, plan, target) for a fully-valid method (no short-circuit), so 2 methods x 4 gates = 8 run, all 8 pass. Re-derived by summing GateResult.passed over run_all_gates(m) for each example method.
total_gates_passed.script: PYTHONPATH=<methods_paper>:<methods_paper>/src python3 -c 'from src.methods_dsl import all_example_methods, run_all_gates; print(sum(sum(1 for g in run_all_gates(m) if g.passed) for m in all_example_methods()))'

total_gates_run.reason: Total staged-gate evaluations run across both methods ({{TOTAL_GATES_RUN}}). 2 methods x 4 gates = 8. Both worked examples are constructed to pass every gate (structural, semantic, plan, target), so run_all_gates does not short-circuit.
total_gates_run.script: PYTHONPATH=<methods_paper>:<methods_paper>/src python3 -c 'from src.methods_dsl import all_example_methods, run_all_gates; print(sum(len(run_all_gates(m)) for m in all_example_methods()))'

trust_chain_length.reason: Length of the demonstration provenance hash-chain ({{TRUST_CHAIN_LENGTH}}): calibration_offset threaded through DECLARED -> CALIBRATED -> VERIFIED tiers = 3 records. Re-derived via demo_chain_report()['chain_length'].

calibration_plan_hash.claim_text: | `SensorCalibrationSweep` | {{CALIBRATION_STEP_COUNT}} | `{{CALIBRATION_TARGET}}` | `{{CALIBRATION_PLAN_HASH}}` |  (rendered CALIBRATION_PLAN_HASH = d89cced19be6, full digest pinned here)

total_gates_passed.claim_text: the analysis script tallies {{TOTAL_GATES_PASSED}} of {{TOTAL_GATES_RUN}} staged-gate evaluations passing (run_all_gates x {{EXAMPLE_METHOD_COUNT}} methods x {{DSL_GATE_COUNT}} gates each)  (rendered 8 of 8)

total_gates_run.claim_text: {{TOTAL_GATES_PASSED}} of {{TOTAL_GATES_RUN}} staged-gate evaluations passing  (rendered TOTAL_GATES_RUN = 8 = 2 methods x 4 gates)

trust_chain_length.claim_text: appends a {{TRUST_CHAIN_LENGTH}}-record demonstration hash-chain for one value (calibration_offset) through DECLARED -> CALIBRATED -> VERIFIED tiers  (rendered TRUST_CHAIN_LENGTH = 3)
